full_response:
Step 1. Write down the coefficients.

F(x) = ∑_{k=-5}^{5} f_k x^{k}, with
f_{-5}=1, f_{-4}=2, f_{-3}=3, f_{-2}=4, f_{-1}=5,
f_0=6, f_1=7, f_2=8, f_3=9, f_4=10, f_5=11.

G(x) = ∑_{ℓ=-3}^{3} g_ℓ x^{ℓ}, with
g_{-3}=1, g_{-2}=2, g_{-1}=3, g_0=4, g_1=5, g_2=6, g_3=7.

P(x) = ∑_{i=0}^{10} p_i x^{i}, where p_i=i+1 (so p_0=1,…,p_{10}=11).

Q(x) = ∑_{j=0}^{6} q_j x^{j}, where q_j=j+1 (so q_0=1,…,q_6=7).

For any exponent t, the coefficient of x^{t} in F·G is  

  c_FG(t)=∑_{k+ℓ=t} f_k g_ℓ.  

Likewise, the coefficient of x^{t} in P·Q is  

  c_PQ(t)=∑_{i+j=t} p_i q_j.

Step 2. Compute the four requested coefficients.

A. Constant term (t=0).

 F·G: k ranges from –3 to 3 (because ℓ=–k must be in [–3,3]).  
 c_FG(0)=3·7+4·6+5·5+6·4+7·3+8·2+9·1=140.

 P·Q: only i=j=0 possible.  
 c_PQ(0)=1·1=1.

 140≠1  ⇒  statement A is false.

B. Coefficient of x¹ (t=1).

 Admissible k: –2,…,4 (because 1–k∈[–3,3]).  
 c_FG(1)=4·7+5·6+6·5+7·4+8·3+9·2+10·1  
    =28+30+30+28+24+18+10=168.

 P·Q: pairs (i,j)=(0,1),(1,0).  
 c_PQ(1)=1·2+2·1=4.

 168≠4  ⇒  statement B is false.

C. Coefficient of x² in F·G versus x¹⁰ in P·Q.

 F·G, t=2, k=–1,…,5.  
 c_FG(2)=5·7+6·6+7·5+8·4+9·3+10·2+11·1  
    =35+36+35+32+27+20+11=196.

 P·Q, t=10, j=0…6 (so i=10–j).  
 c_PQ(10)=11·1+10·2+9·3+8·4+7·5+6·6+5·7  
      =11+20+27+32+35+36+35=196.

 196=196  ⇒  statement C is true.

D. Coefficient of x^{–2} in F·G versus x⁶ in P·Q.

 F·G, t=–2, k=–5,…,1.  
 c_FG(–2)=1·7+2·6+3·5+4·4+5·3+6·2+7·1  
     =7+12+15+16+15+12+7=84.

 P·Q, t=6, j=0…6 (i=6–j).  
 c_PQ(6)=7·1+6·2+5·3+4·4+3·5+2·6+1·7  
     =7+12+15+16+15+12+7=84.

 84=84  ⇒  statement D is true.

Step 3. Conclusion.

Only statements C and D are correct.

\boxed{\text{C and D are true; A and B are false}}

Confidence: 92%
extracted_answer: \text{C and D are true; A and B are false}
